verdict_reason: no treatment variable loaded; missing: ['oecd:entrepreneurship_at_a_glance_high_growth_firms', 'oecd:venture_capital_invested', 'constructed: state_aid + subsidies + directed_credit_guarantees']
falsification_rule_text: Not supported if (a) the startup-density coefficient is not positive and significant at p<0.05, OR (b) in the horse-race the industrial-policy-spending coefficient has a larger absolute t-statistic than startup density, OR (c) the top-quartile vs bottom-quartile productivity growth gap is below 0.20 pp/year. A developmentalist / industrial-policy reading wins cleanly if (b) holds — i.e. state-directed spending outperforms private startup formation in predicting prosperity.
